ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general milk tea entry; no specific entry for 3-in-1 instant premixed format. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code for instant premixed 3-in-1 milk tea format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for instant premixed milk tea beverage.

allergens: milk (non-dairy creamer may contain milk derivatives)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (approximately 18–20 g powder) dissolved in 180–200 ml of hot water, yielding roughly 200 g of prepared beverage.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Flavoured Milk Tea — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for '3 in 1 flavoured milk tea' (cr_id D09020002). All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 instant flavoured milk tea in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened milk tea / tea with sugar and milk, which falls in the medium GI range (60–70) due to sucrose and maltodextrin from non-dairy creamer. Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 x 15.92) / 100 = 10.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar (4.67 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB); palm-based non-dairy creamer contributes saturated fat with no compensating potassium or fibre benefit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this SSB and choose plain unsweetened tea; the low sodium content (22 mg/100 g) is not a concern, but the added sugar and saturated fat from palm-based creamer contribute to cardiovascular risk without any compensating nutrients such as potassium or dietary fibre.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with added sugar (4.67 g/100 g) and medium GI (65); non-dairy creamer may contain maltodextrin which further elevates glycaemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage entirely, as it is classified as an SSB with added sugar and a medium glycaemic index of approximately 65; the non-dairy creamer may contain maltodextrin which further raises the glycaemic load, and no fibre or protein is present in sufficient quantity to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Palm-based non-dairy creamer contributes saturated fat (~1.6 g/100 g); added sugar contributes to dyslipidaemia risk; no beneficial MUFA, PUFA, fibre, or potassium present
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage due to its palm-based saturated fat content from non-dairy creamer and added sugar, both of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles; switching to plain unsweetened black or green tea would eliminate these risks while preserving potential polyphenol benefits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Instant Flavoured Milk Tea is an ultra-processed instant beverage popular in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It comes as a single-serve sachet containing a pre-blended powder of black tea extract, non-dairy creamer (typically palm oil-based), sugar, and additional flavourings. Preparation requires only dissolving the sachet contents in hot water, making it a convenient breakfast or mid-morning drink. The resulting beverage is sweet, milky, and lightly tea-flavoured with a smooth, slightly creamy texture. Per 100 g of prepared beverage, it provides 50 kcal, 0.29 g protein, 1.89 g fat (predominantly saturated from palm-based creamer), 7.96 g carbohydrate, and 4.67 g sugar. Sodium is low at 22 mg/100 g. Dietary fibre is minimal at 0.44 g/100 g. Clinically, this product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its added sugar content, medium glycaemic index (~65), and pro-inflammatory saturated fat from palm-based creamer. It offers no meaningful micronutrient benefit. Consumers seeking a healthier alternative should choose plain unsweetened tea.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics drink instant milk tea?
How many calories in a sachet of 3-in-1 milk tea?
Is premixed milk tea high in sugar?
Is instant milk tea suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020002) at confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, clearly documented. All mandatory multilingual names for Singaporean cuisine are present.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at 1.60 g/100 g based on typical palm-based non-dairy creamer formulation; HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry — value is an informed estimate consistent with the fat profile of palm oil creamer products. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, and all vitamin/mineral fields except calcium and iron are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, potassium, cholesterol, and micronutrient data for this instant premixed beverage are not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo D09020002). water_g set to -1: moisture content of the prepared beverage is not reported in the HPB source. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated for a 200 g prepared serving: GL = (65 x 15.92) / 100 = 10.3.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name '3-in-1 Instant Flavoured Milk Tea' is precise. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names present: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (50.0), protein_g (0.29), fat_g (1.89), carbohydrate_g (7.96) all present and sourced from HPB Foodo D09020002 at confidence 0.95.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.44), sodium_mg (22.0), saturated_fat_g (1.60), sugar_g (4.67) all present as numeric values. Saturated fat is an informed estimate documented in notes.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (65), gi_category (medium), glycemic_load_per_serving (10.3), gi_source all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database framework, clearly documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn close match to milk tea URI provided. FoodEx2 A0FKM (tea-based beverages) used as closest defensible code. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, covers food identity, preparation, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name correct), Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array. Coverage includes English variants, Chinese script, Malay variants, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized form.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Rebuilt clean valid JSON from scratch, eliminating the runaway duplicate Tamil alias array that caused JSON parse failure in iteration 2.
Trimmed aliases to a clean, non-duplicated set of 13 distinct entries covering all required languages.
Verified aliases_by_language consistency against flat aliases array before output.
Added saturated_fat_g estimate with documentation in notes.
Added GI analogous estimate with full source documentation.
Added -1 field documentation in notes per g3 rule.
Added confirmed zero documentation for calcium_mg and iron_mg per g4/g3 rules.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all 6 hard gates pass and all 8 quality criteria satisfied. Nutrient data sourced from HPB Foodo (exact match). GI is a documented analogous estimate. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names present. Aliases consistent between flat array and structured object. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
